nodes.context: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Rockford, Illinois, active c. 1920–2005.
Powerful Mafia crime family based in Chicago, Illinois.
Mafia crime family based in New York City.
Mafia crime family based in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.
Mafia crime family based in Springfield, Illinois.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1960, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) confirmed that the Rockford family had remained an independent crime family, even though they were within close distance to the powerful Chicago Outfit.
edges.context: The Rockford family was independent but geographically close to the Chicago Outfit.

edges.evidence_quote: During the late 1950s, many associates of the Chicago Outfit began relocating to Rockford to join Zammuto's Mafia family.
edges.context: Chicago Outfit associates moved to Rockford to join the Rockford family.

edges.evidence_quote: It was concluded by the FBI that Ferraro did not know the boss of the Rockford family, confirming that there was little connection between the two crime families.
edges.context: FBI concluded there was little connection between the two families.

edges.evidence_quote: As boss Zammuto maintained close ties and friendships with Chicago Outfit boss Joseph Aiuppa
edges.context: Rockford boss Zammuto had close ties with Chicago Outfit boss Aiuppa.

edges.evidence_quote: As boss Zammuto maintained close ties and friendships with ... Milwaukee family boss Frank Balistrieri.
edges.context: Rockford boss Zammuto had close ties with Milwaukee boss Balistrieri.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, the FBI revealed that Vince had become Rockford's underboss and had been working with Milwaukee family boss Frank Balistrieri in a number of illegal rackets.
edges.context: Rockford underboss Vince worked with Milwaukee boss Balistrieri in illegal rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Gulotta maintained close friendships with Zammuto and Milwaukee boss Frank Balistrieri.
edges.context: Rockford boss Gulotta had close friendships with Milwaukee boss Balistrieri.

edges.evidence_quote: Zammuto's consigliere Joseph Zito was a brother to the Springfield Mafia boss Frank Zito.
edges.context: Rockford consigliere Joseph Zito was brother to Springfield boss Frank Zito.

edges.evidence_quote: He also kept close ties and friendship with Bonanno family capo Anthony "Tony" Riela, from New Jersey.
edges.context: Rockford boss Vince had close ties with Bonanno capo Riela.

edges.evidence_quote: With the support of the Chicago Outfit longtime Rockford member Frank "Gumba" Saladino became the boss.
edges.context: Chicago Outfit supported Saladino becoming Rockford boss.

edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, Saladino associated himself with the Chicago Outfit's South Side/26th Street crew in committing many contract murders for the Chicago Outfit.
edges.context: Rockford member Saladino associated with Chicago Outfit crew for contract murders.

edges.evidence_quote: On April 25, 2005, the U.S. Department of Justice launched Operation Family Secrets which indicted Frank Saladino alongside thirteen Chicago Outfit members and associates under the RICO Act
edges.context: Rockford boss Saladino was indicted alongside Chicago Outfit members in Operation Family Secrets.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno crime family , Chicago Outfit , Milwaukee crime family , Springfield crime family
edges.context: Infobox lists Bonanno crime family as an ally.

edges.context: Infobox lists Chicago Outfit as an ally.
